text.title: Agent Trajectory Eval Method Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules every trajectory-eval run enforces. Hard rules — agent output cannot relax them.

rule.statement: The golden trajectory set is versioned and frozen for the duration of any comparison. Adding entries mid-run invalidates the comparison.

rationale.source: ML reproducibility / Pineau et al. 2020 Reproducibility Checklist

rule.rationale: Mutable eval sets produce non-comparable metrics; the denominator changes silently.

rule.statement: Every run reports three layers: system_efficiency {latency_ms, tokens, tool_calls}, session_quality {trajectory_exact_match, trajectory_precision, trajectory_recall}, node_precision {tool_selection_accuracy, param_accuracy}. Skipping any layer is rejected.

rationale.source: Vertex AI trajectory metrics; TRACE arXiv:2602.21230

rule.rationale: Single-layer scores hide failure modes — a high pass-rate with awful trajectory metrics is the usual production-incident profile.

rule.statement: Each layer reports mean AND 95% CI. Naked numbers are rejected.
rule.rationale: 0.82 ± 0.08 on n=30 is a different decision than 0.82 ± 0.01 on n=1000.

rule.statement: If trajectory_quality is LLM-judged, the judge is recalibrated against ≥20 human-labelled trajectories per quarter. Drift in the judge invalidates downstream metrics.
rule.rationale: Judge models change with provider releases; uncalibrated judges measure themselves, not the target.

rule.statement: Cost per eval run is logged. Runs costing more than 2× the production daily traffic are downsampled or moved to nightly.
rule.rationale: Unbounded eval cost breaks the cost case and reduces run frequency, defeating the purpose.

example.title: Versioned trajectory eval report
example.code: set: golden-trajectory-v3-frozen-2026-05-15 (n=120) agent_version: support-agent v3.1.0 system_efficiency: latency 4.2s ± 0.3 | tokens 8200 ± 600 | tool_calls 3.8 ± 0.5 session_quality: traj_exact 0.61 ± 0.07 | traj_precision 0.84 ± 0.04 | traj_recall 0.79 ± 0.05 node_precision: tool_sel 0.92 ± 0.03 | params 0.87 ± 0.04 cost: $4.20 (limit $20)
example.note: All three layers reported, every number has a CI, cost present.

example.title: Naked output-only score
example.note: No trajectory layer, no CI, no set version. Trajectory regressions are invisible.
